events.title: WHO and Brazil urge world leaders to finalise Pandemic Agreement to prevent future global health crises
events.description: Global officials are calling on world leaders to finalise a crucial international agreement aimed at preventing future pandemics, according to a joint letter issued on Monday.

events.title: Half of world’s children exposed to at least three climate hazards, Unicef says
events.description: Almost every child, including those from high-income countries, is now exposed to at least one hazard Half of the world’s children are exposed to at least three overlapping climate hazards threatening their health, education and survival, according to a Unicef report. Globally, children face increasing threats from heatwaves, storms, floods and droughts as the climate crisis worsens, with more than one billion facing at least three of these at once. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Half of world’s children exposed to at least three climate hazards, Unicef says

events.title: Violent attacks on schools, pupils and staff around the world up by 40%, says study
events.description: Cases reported in 83 countries, with at least 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted or arrested, GCPEA says Attacks on education globally have surged by 40% with more than 8,556 recorded incidents and 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted, arrested or otherwise harmed in 2024 and 2025, according to new research. Attacks were reported in 83 countries, with the highest incidences recorded in Colombia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, Haiti, Palestine and Ukraine. Continue reading...

events.title: Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts
events.description: As Sudan’s conflict enters a fourth year, civilians are increasingly trapped not only by frontline violence but by fear, disappearance and detention, according to an update by the Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for the Sudan to the 62nd Session of the UN Human Rights Council in Geneva on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts

events.title: Ukraine: Latest Russian attack kills civilians, damages cultural landmark
events.description: Several civilians were killed and dozens more were injured in the latest wave of overnight attacks in Ukraine that targeted the capital Kyiv, the city of Kharkiv and the country’s history and cultural heritage, the United Nations said on Monday.

events.title: Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks
events.description: A Guardian analysis reveals how most of 39 countries facing US entry restrictions are most vulnerable environmentally ‘Every day it’s more barriers’: how the US is shutting out climate refugees Donald Trump ’s immigration crackdown is largely targeting people from the countries most vulnerable to displacement from climate-driven disasters, a Guardian analysis shows. As the Trump administration pushes policies to boost planet-heating fossil fuels , millions of people are being forced to flee their homelands due to storms, floods and droughts worsened by the climate crisis . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks

events.title: Super powers in the 21st century 'often not able' to defeat smaller powers
events.description: Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Peter Zalmayev, Director of the Eurasia Democracy Initiative. His central argument is that traditional assumptions about military strength are increasingly being challenged by technological innovation, asymmetric warfare, and political resilience. In this framework, Ukraine's ability to strike deep into Russian territory and sustain resistance against a much larger adversary serves as evidence that military size alone no longer guarantees strategic success. He suggests that the conflict's ultimate resolution will depend less on battlefield maps than on political calculations in Moscow, Washington, and Kyiv, as well as on the ability of the international community to create a durable framework for Ukrainian sovereignty.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Super powers in the 21st century 'often not able' to defeat smaller powers

events.title: Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children
events.description: The odyssey begins in Indonesia and ends, 15,000 kilometres later, in Kabul, the capital of Afghanistan. Along the way, critical food aid destined for young Afghan students crosses nine countries, over land and sea, skirting geopolitical unrest and conflict zones.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children

events.title: 'Now is the moment':  G7 powers push Russia to end Ukraine war
events.description: G7 leaders agreed on Tuesday to intensify pressure on Russia to end more than four years of war against Ukraine, with US President Donald Trump saying Moscow should "make a deal". Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky joined the summit at the French resort of Evian-les-Bains, with Ukraine seen as holding up well on the battlefield but its cities still the target of deadly Russian attacks in a conflict that has now lasted longer than World War I.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Now is the moment':  G7 powers push Russia to end Ukraine war

events.title: Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children
events.description: Drought, extreme heat and heatwaves are the most prevalent trio of hazards endangering millions of children globally, warned a newly released climate report by the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children

events.title: While world waits for details on Iran-US accord, UN calls for Hormuz aid corridor
events.description: As representatives of Iran and the United States reportedly prepared to sign a new peace agreement at the end of the week, the UN on Monday stressed the urgent need to open an aid corridor to transit the choked-off Strait of Hormuz and prevent a global hunger crisis.

events.title: Christian leaders hold emergency summit in Jerusalem to confront global rise in antisemitism
events.description: International Christian Embassy Jerusalem is hosting an emergency summit addressing rising antisemitism and replacement theology amid church leaders' silence.

events.title: Drone-making workshops take off in Paris
events.description: Are Europeans ready for war? It’s a question that has been asked increasingly often as Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine has dragged on, now lasting longer than the First World War. The threat of future attacks on European soil has prompted a growing interest in new defence technologies – particularly drones. Young people and older generations alike have taken to getting hands-on, attending drone-making workshops, including one which was held in Paris recently.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Drone-making workshops take off in Paris

events.title: WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation
events.description: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has praised Uganda’s response to an Ebola outbreak that has spread from neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while warning that continued vigilance and cross-border cooperation will be critical to stopping transmission.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation

events.title: World News in Brief: State-supported safe houses in Haiti, EU pact strengthens refugee protection, demand for ‘critical minerals’ intensifies
events.description: Haiti has opened its first State-supported safe house for survivors of sexual violence with support from the UN, marking a significant step in responding to the growing crisis affecting women and girls.

events.title: Is the Iran war weakening the US' superpower status?
events.description: The US is still unrivaled in its ability to project military strength around the world, but Iran's success in blocking the Strait of Hormuz has raised tough question on America's role as protector of global trade.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Is the Iran war weakening the US' superpower status?

events.title: Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears
events.description: The Security Council on Friday weighed the future of the UN mechanism responsible for completing the remaining work of the international tribunals for Rwanda and the former Yugoslavia, as members debated how to preserve its legacy while bringing its mandate to an orderly close.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears

events.title: Women and girls caught up in Yemen’s ‘forgotten crisis’ bear the heaviest toll as funding falls
events.description: Yemen remains gripped by one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises, with 22 million – out of a population of 35 million – requiring assistance. Women and girls account for half of those in need, and two-thirds of them are of childbearing age, placing reproductive health at the heart of the emergency.

events.title: 'First line of defence' has collapsed in biggest ever outbreak of rare Ebola strain
events.description: The number of Ebola infections in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) is likely far higher than official figures, Oxfam has warned, due to lack of clean water and sanitation.

events.title: Kenyan student Sheila Chebii fell 15 floors to her death while working at a Sydney hotel. Her family want answers
events.description: A month after the tragedy, mystery surrounds the death of the ‘cheerful’ 25-year-old who came to Australia to pursue her dreams Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast It was meant to be Sheila Chebii’s last shift as a housekeeper. For weeks, the 25-year-old international student had stripped linen, made beds and refilled towels in the rooms of a luxury high-rise hotel above central Sydney. Continue reading...

events.title: Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds
events.description: Amazon and Sony among firms that may have sourced coltan, used in phones, from supply chains controlled by the M23 rebels, says Global Witness Leading global brands including Amazon, Ericsson and Sony are “likely” to have sourced minerals linked to a militia accused of widespread sexual violence, summary executions and torture, a new investigation claims. The companies allegedly, but unknowingly, acquired coltan smuggled from mines in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) that are occupied by the M23 militia , which has committed myriad atrocities in eastern DRC. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds

events.title: Toronto police say dozens of shootings are linked to ‘multilayered’ gun-for-hire network
events.description: Young adults and teens are being recruited through apps like Telegram and paid to carry out attacks, officials say Police investigators in Toronto have said that dozens of shootings – including one at the US consulate in March – are linked to a “multilayered” gun-for-hire network that is also responsible for attacks on synagogues around Canada’s largest city. Toronto’s police chief, Myron Demkiw, told reporters on Tuesday that young adults and teenagers are being recruited through encrypted messaging apps such as Signal, Telegram and WhatsApp by “bad actors” and paid by the networks to carry out the attacks. Shooters are required to film their attacks in order to get paid. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Toronto police say dozens of shootings are linked to ‘multilayered’ gun-for-hire network

events.title: One Nation’s health policies based on misconceptions and may cost the taxpayer, experts warn
events.description: Former Howard-era adviser says Pauline Hanson’s party is channelling Donald Trump’s health agenda Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Medical experts have blasted One Nation’s health policies, saying they do not make sense, are based on misconceptions and could cost taxpayers more money while leaving vulnerable Australians without access to care. The party has promised to withdraw Australia from the World Health Organization and to scrap regulator the Therapeutic Goods Administration, rolling its “essential functions” into the health department. It has also proposed adding photo ID to Medicare cards. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump 'doesn't share European view about Russian threat', analyst says
events.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Ian Lesser, Distinguished Fellow and Advisor to the President of the German Marshall Fund of the United States, explains that Trump "doesn't completely share the European view about the nature of the threat to European security that Russian behaviour poses. Fot him this is a conflict to be gotten over, to be moved off the table and if Europe can take the lead in doing that, so much the better".

events.title: In post-Assad Syria, displaced people await reconstruction
events.description: Syria has been particularly hard hit by the decline in global humanitarian aid, especially from the US. In the northwest of the country, hundreds of thousands of displaced people are still living in makeshift shelters. When the Assad regime fell, they hoped to be able to return home. But on seeing their villages destroyed, most have remained stranded in camps, waiting for reconstruction that will take years. As time goes by, living conditions there are deteriorating and vital infrastructure such as hospitals are at risk of closing.

events.title: Global fish production hits new high, but climate threats loom
events.description: Aquaculture now supplies most of the fish people eat, a landmark FAO report reveals, but rising temperatures and overfishing are putting the future of the industry at risk.

events.title: Refugee turned peacemaker: The young woman leader transforming communities in the Central African Republic

events.title: Starbucks Korea to temporarily shut all stores for history lesson after bungled coffee promotion
events.description: The closures, so employees can watch a recorded lecture, will cost the company an estimated 2.1bn won ($1.4m) in sales Starbucks Korea will simultaneously close all its stores for a mandatory history lesson, after a disastrous promotion that evoked memories of a pro-democracy massacre sparked public and political backlash . More than 2,000 stores will temporarily close at 3pm on 22 June, the company said, so staff can watch recorded lectures on modern Korean history and engage in “social sensitivity” training. The half-day closures will cost Starbucks an estimated 2.1bn won ($1.4m) in lost sales, according to data firm IGAWorks. Continue reading...

events.title: World Cup: Cape Verde hero and Iran suffer US travel woes

events.title: 'Belief has plummeted' among Brazil fans after tricky World Cup start
events.description: Before the World Cup began, one in three Brazilians thought their team would lift the trophy, according to one poll. But after struggling to a 1-1 draw against Morocco in their opening match, confidence has plummeted, says France 24's correspondent in Rio Jan Onoszko.

events.title: Pakistani military aircraft crashes during training flight, killing 2 pilots

events.title: Mexican president says FIFA should reflect on high World Cup ticket prices

events.title: Fujitsu chair resigns after ‘woman-related inappropriate conduct’

The company said on Tuesday that Hidenori Furuta had stepped down after two years in the role. Continue reading...

events.title: French startup unveils non-humanoid robot as AI race moves to physical machines
events.description: French robotics startup Genesis AI on Tuesday unveiled "Eno", its first general-purpose robot, marking a step toward bringing advanced AI from online chatbots into physical machines. Backed by former Google CEO Eric Schmidt, the company says the wheeled robot is designed to extend human capabilities rather than mimic human form, with commercial deployments planned from late 2026.

events.title: Ukraine back in favour? At G7, Trump threatens return of Russia oil sanctions
events.description: Before he left Washington, there were reports Volodymyr Zelenskiy wasn't even on Donald Trump's G7 bilateral meeting list, but in the end, the U-S president took the meeting. And more. In the French Alps resort of Evian, Trump putting the onus on Vladimir Putin to come to the bargaining table and even suggesting a rapid return to full sanctions on Russian oil. Why the sudden turn-for-the-better in relations between Washington and Kyiv?

events.title: Congo reports record one-day increase in Ebola cases, month after outbreak's reveal

events.title: Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies
events.description: Outpouring of public grief for Lidia ‘Taty’ Almeida, leader of group of mothers that has marched every week since 1977 The human rights activist Lidia “Taty” Almeida – who spent more than half a century searching for her son after he was forcibly disappeared by Argentina’s military junta – has died aged 95, prompting a public outpouring of grief. Almeida, 95, was the president of the Mothers of Plaza de Mayo, made up of women who have marched around the square outside Argentina’s presidential palace every Thursday since 1977 , demanding the return of children who were disappeared during the country’s 1976-1983 dictatorship. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Woman, 21, dies after being thrown from Brazil rope jump bridge without harness
events.description: Instructors hurled Maria Eduarda Rodrigues de Freitas into 40-metre abyss without attaching safety equipment A 21-year-old woman who died when two rope-jumping instructors threw her from a bridge without first harnessing her to security equipment has been buried in Brazil ’s São Paulo state. Maria Eduarda Rodrigues de Freitas was rope jumping on Saturday at Ponte do Esqueleto, an abandoned bridge in the municipality of Limeira where tourists practise extreme sports. The young woman, who aspired to become a physical education teacher, had asked to be launched from the bridge airplane-style, with two instructors hoisting her above their shoulders as she spread out her arms. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Woman, 21, dies after being thrown from Brazil rope jump bridge without harness

events.title: European Parliament approves US trade deal to lower tariffs
events.description: The European Parliament has approved cutting tariffs on many US imports to comply with a deal signed last year. US President Trump had given the bloc a July 4 deadline to meet its side of the deal.

events.title: Mike Waltz says Gulf allies back Trump’s Iran pressure campaign after regional trip: ‘Zero daylight’

events.title: Optics of peace first, details later: The US-Iran 60-day challenge
events.description: Negotiators have yet to hold talks on key issues that have plagued US-Iran diplomacy for decades, say analysts.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: JR's "The Cave" on Paris' Pont Neuf is finally open to the public
events.description: "The Cave" at Pont Neuf has finally opened its doors this Monday. The announcement was made by the artist JR in the afternoon, as repairs were nearing completion after the canvas was torn by strong winds on June 2. It is now possible to explore, free of charge and 24 hours a day, the interior of the ephemeral artwork by the artist who became famous for his monumental photographic collage projects and trompe-l'oeil.

events.title: US-Iran: Uncertainty over Iran deal looms large at G7 summit

events.title: Sweden requires public workers to report migrants not authorized to live there

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?

events.title: Pont Neuf 'Cavern' installation over the Seine finally opens after damage
events.description: Visitors are finally stepping into darkness at the Pont Neuf, where French artist JR has transformed Paris’s oldest bridge into a towering artificial cavern. The installation was originally set to open to the public on June 6, but strong winds tore parts of the inflatable structure a few days before its début. Combining scent, sound, photography and augmented reality, the free installation invites people to experience one of the city’s most familiar landmarks in an entirely new way.

events.title: Three children shot near public pool in small Arkansas town, suspect taken into custody
events.description: Three children were shot near a public pool in Stuttgart, Arkansas, authorities said. A suspect was taken into custody within minutes of the incident.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: Canada eliminates human rights watchdog that oversees companies operating abroad
events.description: Mark Carney says Canadian Ombudsperson for Responsible Enterprise office hasn’t been ‘effective’ since its 2019 setup Canada is eliminating a watchdog that investigates alleged human rights violations committed by Canadian companies operating abroad, after Mark Carney said the office hadn’t been “effective” since it was set up in 2019. The move comes as Canada faces criticism from Donald Trump’s administration over its “unacceptable” efforts to combat forced labour. Continue reading...

events.title: ATO outsource call centre workers paid 40% less than public service peers, Fair Work submission claims
events.description: Ahead of ‘same job, same pay’ hearings, former call centre worker Nathan Brunne says pay gap is structural and widens at senior levels Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Workers at the Australian Taxation Office’s outsource call centres are paid up to 40% less than their public service counterparts on the same phone lines, according to submissions lodged ahead of landmark “same job, same pay” hearings. The pay gap, detailed by Nathan Brunne, a former worker on the ATO phone lines employed by the private equity-backed Probe Operations, widens at more senior call centre roles, with team leaders at outsource operators paid about $31 an hour compared with more than $52 at the tax office. Continue reading...

events.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Israel's ambassador to the U.S. Michael Leiter about the peace deal the Trump administration says it's made with Iran.

events.title: G7 leaders meet for first full day of summit Tuesday

events.title: U.K. announces social media ban for children under 16
events.description: The UK government has announced it will ban social media for all children under 16. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was "giving children their childhood back."

events.title: Crude oil is cheaper as markets embrace news of a U.S.-Iran deal
events.description: Crude oil prices are falling as oil markets react to President Trump saying the U.S. and Iran have agreed on a framework for reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: CRISPR gene-editing for crops: Precision tool or new risk?
events.description: The EU is poised to ease restrictions on crops developed using novel genetic techniques. While supporters say the technology could help farmers adapt to climate change, critics argue its effects remain poorly understood.

events.title: G7: Trump says Russia should 'make a deal' on Ukraine
events.description: US President Donald Trump said he has a "good" meeting with Ukraine's Zelenskyy on the sidelines of the G7 summit, as European leaders look to add pressure on Russia. DW has the latest.

events.title: Germany: 25 years since Nazi forced labor compensation began
events.description: A German fund set up to compensate the millions forced to work for the Nazi regime is marking 25 years since the first payments were made. But for many victims, it was much too late.

events.title: How Germany and Poland rebooted relations 35 years ago
events.description: When Germany and Poland signed the Treaty on Good Neighborliness, Friendship and Cooperation in June 1991, they chose partnership over enmity and became close NATO and EU allies. But it hasn't all been plain sailing.

events.title: Diplomats and abuse — chipping at the shield of immunity
events.description: Domestic workers who were mistreated by diplomats are often denied justice due to their abusers enjoying diplomatic immunity. But court rulings in the UK and Switzerland have paved the way for change.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Diplomats and abuse — chipping at the shield of immunity

events.title: Germany news: Population shrinks for first time since 2020
events.description: A dip in net migration meant it could not cancel out Germany's low birth rates in 2025. Meanwhile, fuel prices fell amid news of a possible deal between the US and Iran. And Merz gives Trump a Germany jersey. More on DW.

events.title: Sigh of relief in Africa as the Strait of Hormuz 'reopens'
events.description: A potential US-Iran agreement could lower energy-, fertilizer- and food prices in Africa if the Strait of Hormuz fully reopens. Oil exporters such as Nigeria and Angola might have to cope with less revenue, experts say.

events.title: Charging ahead: EVs outpace growth predictions
events.description: Electric cars are getting cheaper, more efficient and can travel farther than ever. China is driving the transition, but Europe and other countries are catching up fast.

events.title: Football fashion: Has France already won?
events.description: In this edition of Entre Nous we take a deep dive into French football fashion. For many, Les Bleus are already the champions of a de facto sartorial competition, particularly on the Clairefontaine "catwalk", when the players head to their training ground. We also discuss the rich history and many rules regarding their FFF, Nike and Jacquemus kits, as fans and collectors are already lining up for the shirts.

events.title: Graham Coxon on Blur, Brexit and his back catalogue
events.description: In this edition of arts24, we meet a musical polymath and an icon of British popular music. Graham Coxon is one of the founding members of Blur, pioneers of the Britpop movement that dominated the 1990s music scene. A solo artist in his own right, Coxon has released nine studio albums and three soundtracks over the last two decades. Now for the first time, he is re-releasing his entire solo catalogue as well as a new record, "Castle Park", which had been locked away in the musical vaults for more than 15 years. Our Music Editor Jennifer Ben Brahim sat down with him in Paris.

events.title: Arlindo Chissale, a disappearance in the shadows of Mozambique's security services
events.description: Arlindo Chissale, a journalist and political activist in Mozambique, disappeared without a trace on January 7, 2025, in Cabo Delgado, a region in the grips of an Islamist insurrection. Chissale’s public stance and worrying signs in the days leading up to his disappearance make his loved ones suspect that the Mozambican security forces were involved.

events.title: Arlindo Chissale, the Mozambican activist and journalist who knew too much
events.description: Arlindo Chissale, a journalist and activist who was in contact with the FRANCE 24 Observers team, disappeared on January 7, 2025, in the region of Cabo Delgado, Mozambique, which is the scene of a deadly Islamist insurrection. Chissale spoke out about topics the Mozambican government did not want out in the open – a pursuit that his friends say put him in danger.

events.title: French department store BHV Marais to end Shein partnership after backlash
events.description: French department store BHV Marais will end its partnership with Shein after its operating company sold the landmark Paris outlet to a group of executives. The move follows months of controversy over the fast-fashion retailer’s presence in the store, including outcry over its environmental impact, labour practices and its online sale of childlike sex dolls.

events.title: Stepson of Norway's crown prince jailed for two rapes
events.description: The stepson of Norway's Crown Prince Haakon has been jailed for two rapes - including one in the basement of his family's home.

events.title: Nations in Kenya urge quick execution of a key treaty protecting oceans
events.description: African and Commonwealth nations have called for a swift implementation of a landmark treaty protecting the high seas

events.title: German president says Europe is worried over tensions in the disputed South China Sea
events.description: German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier says Europe is worried over tensions in the disputed South China Sea that could endanger freedom of navigation as happened in the Strait of Hormuz
events.ticker_label: 💥 German president says Europe is worried over tensions in the disputed South China Sea

events.title: Monumental cave art on Paris' oldest bridge finally opens, as the public steps and sniffs inside
events.description: French street artist JR has transformed Paris' oldest bridge, the Pont Neuf, into an artificial cavern

events.title: Uffizi Gallery unveils new arrangement for Botticelli's 'Birth of Venus' and 'Primavera'
events.description: The Uffizi Gallery has repositioned Sandro Botticelli’s masterpieces “The Birth of Venus” and “Primavera.”

events.title: South Africa marks 50 years since Soweto uprising, but challenges linger
events.description: South Africa marks the 50th anniversary of the Soweto uprising by students, a pivotal moment in the fight against apartheid

events.title: Luigi Mangione supporter working for far-left DC mayoral candidate likened to Mamdani
events.description: Makia Green, political director for D.C. mayoral candidate Janeese Lewis George, called for accused assassin Luigi Mangione to be freed and pardoned.

events.title: Teen accused of killing stepsister on Carnival cruise ship ordered detained before murder trial
events.description: Teen accused of killing stepsister Anna Kepner on Carnival cruise ship ordered detained after judge finds no release conditions can protect community.

events.title: Austin Metcalf's father rips former Frisco schools chief as 'spineless' over Karmelo Anthony graduation
events.description: Father of slain teen Austin Metcalf slams former Frisco ISD superintendent, claiming he allowed killer Karmelo Anthony to graduate after the stabbing.

events.title: Six men allegedly kidnapped two people at vacation resort, tortured them at boat ramp: FBI
events.description: Six men allegedly kidnapped two people at gunpoint from Margaritaville Hollywood Beach Resort in Florida, tortured them and demanded money, officials say.

events.title: Stephen A. Smith says 'Karmelo Anthony murdered Austin Metcalf'
events.description: Stephen A. Smith says Karmelo Anthony murdered Austin Metcalf, challenging self-defense claims after the 35-year sentence for the Frisco stabbing.

events.title: President Trump announces Iran peace agreement and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope
events.description: A 21-year-old woman died after instructors launched her from a bridge without attaching safety ropes during a rope-jumping event near São Paulo, Brazil.

events.title: IDF announces elimination of Hezbollah commander behind murder of 5 American soldiers
events.description: Israel eliminates senior Hezbollah commander Ali Musa Daqduq, accused of orchestrating the 2007 kidnapping and murder of five American soldiers.

events.title: Zelenskyy congratulates Trump on turning 80 in birthday call, vows more Ukraine peace talks at G7 on Tuesday
events.description: Zelenskyy congratulated Trump on turning 80 and discussed peace efforts in a phone call ahead of the G7 Summit, where the two leaders agreed to meet.

events.title: Germany pledges to build Europe's strongest army as NATO allies answer Trump pressure
events.description: Germany pledges to become Europe's strongest conventional army inside NATO, with ambassador saying Berlin is ready to assume greater defense role.

events.title: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone flights
events.description: South Korean court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years for allegedly ordering drone flights over North Korea to justify martial law.
